cards.back: A two-dimensional array is a data structure that stores elements in rows and columns.

cards.front: How to declare a two-dimensional array in a programming language?
cards.back: Typically with two set of square brackets: arrayName[rowIndex][columnIndex].

cards.front: What is the purpose of the index in a two-dimensional array?
cards.back: The index is used to access a specific element in the array by specifying the row and column.

cards.front: What is the difference between 2D and 1D arrays?
cards.back: The main difference is that 2D arrays store elements in rows and columns, while 1D arrays store elements in a single sequence.

cards.front: How to access a specific element in a two-dimensional array?
cards.back: To access an element, specify the row index and column index using square brackets: arrayName[rowIndex][columnIndex].
